Summary:
- Harmonized Name: Ratio of Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second to Forced Vital Capacity
- UK Variable: FEV1/FVC
- Source Label / Description: The ratio of the volume of air that can be forcibly blown out in one second, after full inspiration, to the total volume of air that can be forcibly blown out after full inspiration.
- Target Concept: Ratio of Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second to Forced Vital Capacity
- Standard Vocabulary: LOINC
- Standard Concept Code: 19926-5
- Match Confidence: High
- Summary: The UK variable 'FEV1/FVC' maps directly to the LOINC standard concept 'Ratio of Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second to Forced Vital Capacity' with a high level of confidence. This is a common measurement used in pulmonary function tests to diagnose and monitor lung diseases such as asthma.
